quick_tips.edit_desc: Cards have separate display and edit modes for better reading and better editing
quick_tips.edit_mode_instructions:
Cards have separate display and edit modes to give you a better reading and better editing experience.
1. Click on a card to focus on it
2. Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> or Double-click on the card content to go into the 'Edit' mode
3. Press <kbd>Cmd / Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Enter</kbd> to finish your changes render the card in 'Display' mode
quick_tips.flashcard_desc: Learn how to create and use flashcards for effective studying
quick_tips.flashcard_instructions: Turn your notes into flashcards for effective studying & recalling. We use spaced repetition to help you remember your cards over time. 1. Select a card and press Enter to edit 2. Add a horizontal rule - - - to split your card up into two sections, with the question above and the answer below. If no dividers are used, the card title will be used as the initial question and the card body as the answer. 3. Add spoilers in the first section by wrapping text with !! – great for filling in the blanks. 4. Press Alt / OptionF to switch to Flashcards view and begin studying with a smart review schedule (recommended). 5. Mark cards as × Again if you got them wrong, ~ Hard if you only remembered bits, ✓ Good if you got most of it right, and  ✓✓ Easy if you knew it off by heart 6. Your recall probability will be calculated after your first review session – a great insight into how well you know those cards.
quick_tips.link_desc: Creating links between cards is a great way to connect your thoughts together
quick_tips.link_instructions:
Create links between cards to quickly connect related concepts and ideas together, and help you quickly identify where a card has been mentioned before.
1. While in card edit mode, type <kbd>[</kbd><kbd>[</kbd> to open the inline 'Link Coupler', or use the ( + ) button in top right like the example video above.
2. Search for the card you want to link to. Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to add the link to your card, this will create a markdown card link for you wherever your cursor was in the card. As a card link looks just like a normal markdown link, you can even edit the name of the link if you'd like!
3. Clicking on a link when in 'Display' mode will then immediately open that card in the Preview.
4. This will also create backlinks on the card you linked to, so you can easily see all the cards that link to it.
quick_tips.mobile_edit_instructions:
Cards have separate display and edit modes to give you a better reading and better editing experience.
1. Tap on a card to focus on it
2. Double Tap on the card content to go into 'Edit' mode
3. Press the Check on the top right of your keyboard to finish your changes and render the card in 'Display' mode
quick_tips.mobile_flashcard_instructions: Turn your notes into flashcards for effective studying & recalling. We use spaced repetition to help you remember your cards over time. 1. Double tap on a card to enter edit mode 2. Add a horizontal rule - - - to split your card up into two sections, with the question above and the answer below. If no dividers are used, the card title will be used as the initial question and the card body as the answer. 3. Add spoilers in the first section by wrapping text with !! – great for filling in the blanks 4. Press Alt / OptionF to switch to Flashcards view and begin studying 5. Mark cards as × Again if you got them wrong, ~ Hard if you only remembered bits, ✓ Good if you got most of it right, and  ✓✓  Easy if you knew it off by heart
quick_tips.mobile_link_instructions:
Create links between cards to quickly connect related concepts and ideas together, and help you quickly identify where a card has been mentioned before.
1. While in card edit mode, tap the ( + ) plus button just above your keyboard to open the Universal Coupler.
2. Tap the 'Link' option.
3. Search for the card you want to link to. Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to add the link to your card, this will create a markdown card link for you wherever your cursor was in the card. As a card link looks just like a normal markdown link, you can even edit the name of the link if you'd like.
4. When a card is in 'Display' mode, tapping the card link will then immediately open that card in the Preview.
5. This will also create backlinks on the card you linked to, so you can easily see all the cards that link to it.
quick_tips.mobile_parent_instructions:
Once you have more than a handful of cards, it can feel daunting to start organising all of them. Tags are a great help, but you might find that groups of cards work well together. You can group all of these cards inside a single 'parent' card. This process is fundamental for sharing, since sharing a single 'parent' card gives your friends instant real-time access to all the 'child' cards (if they are shared!).
1. While in card edit mode, tap the ( + ) plus button just above your keyboard to open the Universal Coupler.
2. Tap the 'Parent' option or press <kbd>></kbd> to open the Link Coupler.
3. Search for a card you'd like to make a parent of the one you are currently editing. Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to add this parent.
4. Like tags, you will notice you can add parents as 'Shared' or 'Private'. Adding a card privately to a parent means only you will be able to see it. You can switch between shared and private parents using the <kbd>Tab</kbd> key.
5. Unlike folders in a traditional file system, you can add multiple parents to a card. A card can be a child of multiple parents, giving you a lot more flexibility on how to organise your knowledge base.
6. Now you've started to build a hierarchy, which you can see in your Outline in the left hand sidebar. You can open parent cards in the Noteboard and see all the child cards inside them.
quick_tips.mobile_pro_tip_links: While editing, select text that matches the name of another card. And when you open the Universal Coupler, it will automatically open the Link Coupler and search for that card – just press enter to add the link, *super fast*!
quick_tips.mobile_pro_tip_parents: Drag and drop card titles on top of each other, to quickly add cards inside of them.
quick_tips.mobile_tag_instructions:
Tags help you quickly filter and view relevant cards, so its good to get into a habit of using them. With conventional files you might have struggled to see repeating / connecting themes and topics – but with Supernotes you can just tag them! 
1. While in card edit mode, tap the ( + ) plus button just above your keyboard to open the Universal Coupler.
2. Tap on the 'Tag' option.
3. Type a new tag or select a previous one from the list. You can add multiple tags at a time.
4. You will notice you can add shared and private tags. 'Shared' tags mean if you share this card with someone in the future they will be able to see the tags as well. Or if you have a private tag, only you will be able to see it.
5. Once you've finished adding all your tags, finish your card by tappign the check on the top right of your keyboard.
6. Tap on a tag when a card is in 'Display' mode to filter all your cards by that tag.
quick_tips.parent_desc: Parents are a great way to group cards together
quick_tips.parent_instructions:
Once you have more than a handful of cards, it's time to start organising them. Tags are a great help, but you might find that groups of cards work well together. You can group all of these cards inside a single 'parent' card. This process is also fundamental for sharing, since sharing a single 'parent' card gives your friends instant real-time access to all the 'child' cards (if they are shared!).
1. While in card edit mode, type <kbd>></kbd><kbd>></kbd> to open the inline 'Parent Coupler', or use the ( + ) button in top right like the example video above.
2. Search for a card that will become the parent of the one you are currently editing. Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to confirm.
3. By default, the parent card will be added as 'Shared'. This means others can see this card if you share the parent card with them. If you don't plan to share, you can ignore this. If you are in a shared parent and specifically want to make this card private (visible only to you), click the parent badge to change it to 'Private'.
4. Unlike folders in a traditional file system, you can add multiple parents to a card. A card can be a child of multiple parents, giving you a lot more flexibility on how to organise your knowledge base.
5. Now you've started to build a hierarchy, which you can see in your Outline in the left hand sidebar. You can open parent cards in the Noteboard and see all the child cards inside them.
quick_tips.pro_tip_flashcard: Use multiple horizontal rules to create multiple sections, and cycle through them one by one in the Flashcards view.
quick_tips.pro_tip_font: Customize the font size of all your cards, by going into Settings > User Preferences > Accessibility – great if you like to view things a bit bigger or smaller!
quick_tips.pro_tip_links: While editing, select text that matches the name of another card. And when you open the Universal Coupler, it will automatically present the 'Link Coupler' and search for that card – just press enter to add the link, *super fast*!
quick_tips.pro_tip_parents: Drag and drop card titles on top of each other, to quickly add cards inside of them. Hold <kbd>Shift</kbd> while doing this to move a card inside this parent, instead of adding it.
quick_tips.pro_tip_share: Add friends on Supernotes to share cards directly with them, without the need for share links!
quick_tips.pro_tip_tags: Actively filtered tags (and colors / parents) will automatically be added to a new card, great if you're writing a bunch of cards on the same topic in one go
quick_tips.share_desc: Publish a card to a secret link on the web, and share your thoughts with friends who don't have Supernotes (yet!)
quick_tips.share_instructions:
Publish a card to a secret link on the web, and share your thoughts with friends who don't have Supernotes (yet!)
1. Open the 'Share' panel of a card (now located within the '...' button). Here you have two options, you can either create share links or invite friends directly to join this card.
2. Click 'Create a share link' to immediately generate one.
3. Copy this link and send it to anyone you like. You can also set the permissions of the share link, using the '...' button, so you can decide whether you want people to be able to join the card on Supernotes and edit it or just view it.
quick_tips.share_via_web: Share this card via the web
quick_tips.tag_desc: Tags help you quickly sort your cards, so its good to get into a habit of using them
quick_tips.tag_instructions:
Tags help you quickly filter and view relevant cards, so its good to get into a habit of using them. With conventional files you might have struggled to see repeating / connecting themes and topics – but with Supernotes you can just tag them! 
1. While in card edit mode, type <kbd>#</kbd> to open the inline 'Tag Coupler', or use the ( + ) button in top right like the example video above.
2. Type a new tag or select a previous one from the list. You can add multiple tags at a time.
3. You will notice you can add shared and private tags. 'Shared' tags mean if you share this card with someone in the future they will be able to see the tags as well. Or if you have a private tag, only you will be able to see it.
4. Once you've finished adding all your tags, finish your card. 
5. Click on a tag when a card is in 'Display' mode to filter all your cards by that tag.

user.add_bio_prompt: Add a bio here by editing your profile below...
user.add_photo_bio: Add a photo and brief bio.
user.api_keys_description: Here you can manage your API keys used to access Supernotes from 3rd-party clients.
user.automatic_description: The appearance reflects your system settings
user.change_password_description: Enter your current password and then the new password you would like to use.
user.choose_life_area: What part of your life would you most like to improve on?
user.day_description: Manually set the appearance to the day mode
user.delete_account_description: All of your cards will be immediately deleted. Any cards you have created that have been shared with others will also be deleted.
user.delete_account_warning: WARNING this is permanent and cannot be undone!
user.devices_description: See which devices are currently signed in with your account. If there's a device you don't recognize please
user.email_to_supernotes_description: Send an email to the address below and we'll automatically add a corresponding card to your Supernotes library.
user.explore_new_layouts: Explore the new **Table**, **Map** and **Flashcard** Layouts and let us know what you think!
user.high_contrast_off_description: Standard contrast and highlighting for better readability
user.high_contrast_on_description: Highlight focused elements and increase contrast for better interactivity
user.minimize_to_tray_description: Minimise to tray when clicking the close button, keep app running in background
user.night_description: Manually set the appearance to the night mode
user.quit_application_description: Quit the application when clicking the close button, to fully quit the app & processes
user.select_option_continue: Select an option above to continue
user.sync_completed_message: The sync has completed. It is now safe to sign out.
user.unlimited_required: Try out new features before they are released to everyone else. Only available with
user.unsynced_changes_warning: {{count}} {{changes}} being sent – please wait for syncing to complete before signing out. If you sign out now, {{theseChanges}} will be lost.

user_limit.check_progress_servers: To check your progress with our servers
user_limit.complete_challenges_get_free: Complete these challenges, get more free!
user_limit.congrats_earned_all_rewards: Congrats, you've earned all the rewards!
user_limit.junk_cards_free_space: I will junk cards to free up space
user_limit.used_up_all_cards: You've used up all of your {{userQuota}} cards

user_manage_emails.add_additional_emails_description: Add your additional emails, so other members can easily add you as a friend or send you cards.
user_manage_emails.awaiting_confirmation: is still awaiting confirmation. Please check your inbox, or resend a confirmation using the button below.
user_manage_emails.educational_email_discount: If you have an educational email, add it above to unlock the Supernotes educational discount.
user_manage_emails.primary_email_address: is your primary email address, you cannot remove a primary email.
user_manage_emails.valid_educational_email: This is also a valid educational email, and a discount has been applied.

user_preferences_behavior.auto_add_location_disabled_desc: Don't add location data to new cards
user_preferences_behavior.auto_add_location_enabled_desc: Automatically add location data to new cards
user_preferences_behavior.bullet_desc: Daily content will be appended as a bullet list item
user_preferences_behavior.cursor_body_desc: New cards will start with the cursor in the content section
user_preferences_behavior.cursor_title_desc: New cards will start with the cursor in the title section
user_preferences_behavior.day_format_desc: Dates will be formatted as Day/Month/Year
user_preferences_behavior.four_spaces_desc: Use four spaces to indent lines
user_preferences_behavior.horizontal_desc: Columnar view will default to horizontal orientation
user_preferences_behavior.inverted_desc: Switch click behavior so cards are opened on single-click
user_preferences_behavior.long_press_desc: New card menu will be opened on long press
user_preferences_behavior.mermaid_button_desc: Mermaid diagrams will only render when mermaid icon is clicked
user_preferences_behavior.mermaid_inline_desc: Mermaid diagrams will be automatically rendered inline
user_preferences_behavior.month_format_desc: Dates will be formatted as Month/Day/Year
user_preferences_behavior.plain_desc: Content will be appended as plain text
user_preferences_behavior.regular_desc: Use the standard card click behavior
user_preferences_behavior.single_press_desc: New card menu will be opened on single click
user_preferences_behavior.todo_desc: Daily content will be appended as a todo item
user_preferences_behavior.two_spaces_desc: Use two spaces to indent lines
user_preferences_behavior.vertical_desc: Columnar view will default to vertical orientation

validation.no_special_chars: cannot contain periods, colons, or slashes
validation.only_alphanumeric: can only contain letters, numbers, periods, and underscores
validation.password_max_128: password must be less than 128 characters
validation.password_min_8: password must be at least 8 characters
